jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 2+ years of data center, network engineering, and/or systems engineering experience
- 2+ years of experience managing people in a technical environment. "
jobs.description:
This position involves leading teams in hardware and network diagnostics followed by physical repair and includes participation in an on-call rotation. All candidates should be willing to work both independently and managing their teams effectively. Work prioritization, organizational skills, effective communication, and the ability to react quickly are critical to being successful. In addition to strong knowledge in Data Centers, hardware and network repair, candidates will create documentation, innovate solutions, participate in site-level project work, and fix complex problems within the Data Center Space. This team works in an environment that operates 24/7, in addition this role will operate on a night shift schedule that is subject to adjustment to meet operational demands.

Key job responsibilities
The successful candidate will be operationally responsible for a Data Center. Some high-level responsibilities include:
- Prioritize and assign trouble tickets to data center technicians and operators
- Manage the performance of a diverse team(s) of data center operation professionals with varying skills across different locations
- Developing Career Paths for the employees and to make updates in our internal tools.
- Recruit and train data technicians to ensure appropriate staffing levels
- Ensure effective and efficient management of day to day data center operations including queue management, 7/24 shift arrangement and hardware logistics
- Fast learn or act as the subject matter expert across all aspects in data center operations
- Ensure all operational KPIs and metrics are being measured and met
- Inspire and guide improvement in team process, technology innovation and automation
- Manage Large Scale Events (outages) and act as the call leader
- Manage and improve the work-flows and through-put for data centers operations
- Recommend, document, and oversee policies and procedures to meet industry best practices and to meet required SLAs
- Maintain the on-call schedule coordinating absence and vacations
- Be passionate about the quality and quantity of services being provided, and continuously strive to improve our Customer Experience

A day in the life
Amazon Web Services (AWS) is a fast paced technology company and a leader in the world of data centers. The position will help ensure overall availability and reliability to meet or exceed defined service levels of data center operations. We are currently expanding our infrastructure management team and are looking for technology managers with experience in people management, strong technical understanding and the drive and vision to take AWS Data Center Operations to the next level.

About the team
Amazon Web Services (AWS) is a fast paced technology company and a leader in the world of data centers. The position will help ensure overall availability and reliability to meet or exceed defined service levels of data center operations. We are currently expanding our infrastructure management team and are looking for technology managers with experience in people management, strong technical understanding and the drive and vision to take AWS Data Center Operations to the next level.

jobs.title: Data Center Manager

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 3+ years of engineering team management experience
- 7+ years of working directly within engineering teams experience
- 3+ years of designing or architecting (design patterns, reliability and scaling) of new and existing systems experience
- 8+ years of leading the definition and development of multi tier web services experience
- Knowledge of engineering practices and patterns for the full software/hardware/networks development life cycle, including coding standards, code reviews, source control management, build processes, testing, certification, and livesite operations
- Experience partnering with product or program management teams
jobs.description:
Amazon's Last Mile delivery network moves millions of packages every day and the accuracy of the maps powering that network matters at every turn. The Suggested Editing Foundations team sits at the heart of this mission, building the foundational visual geospatial data editing capabilities that power the tools used by delivery partners, dispatchers, and logistics associates to identify and correct geospatial defects in the real world.

As the Software Development Manager for Suggested Editing Foundations, you will lead a team of talented engineers building and scaling the core platform that underpins GeoStudio, Amazon's a web-based geospatial editing suite, and the broader ecosystem of tools that depend on it. You will own systems that generate millions of rendered map tile assets, serve geospatial data to clients at scale, provide reusable components adopted by partner teams across Last Mile and beyond, and an agentic AI assistant that enhances the geospatial editing experience for customers.

This is a role for an operator who enables builders: someone who creates the conditions for a high-performing team to do their best work, removes friction, drives technical clarity, and ensures the team delivers with quality and velocity.

What You'll Own

You will have end-to-end ownership of a portfolio of systems that are foundational to Amazon's geospatial editing capabilities:

- GeoStudio and GeoStudio Components: The primary map editing experience used by DSPs and dispatchers to action on transporter-reported geospatial defects, built as a React-based web application with reusable frontend components, now expanding to new teams and global markets
- GeoStudio Web SDK and Visual Maps Web SDK: Reusable SDKs enabling partner teams to build geospatial editing and visualization experiences across web and mobile
- GeoStudio Assistant: An agentic AI assistant integrated directly into GeoStudio, leveraging large language models to enhance the map editing experience for customers and streamline how users interact with geospatial data
- GeoStudio Backend: Backend services supporting the editing workflow and powering the GeoStudio platform
- Tile Generation and Stylesheets: Systems that conflate multiple map data sources with editor-contributed data to generate millions of rendered map tile assets consumed by every Last Mile associate who uses a map
- Scalable Tier-1 Infrastructure: High-availability APIs and data services enabling clients to fetch geospatial data reliably from multiple sources


Key job responsibilities
- Lead, mentor, and grow a team of software engineers, setting clear performance expectations, providing actionable feedback, and supporting career development. Build an inclusive, high-trust environment where engineers take ownership and make decisions.
- Own and drive the technical roadmap for Suggested Editing Foundations, balancing near-term delivery with long-term platform investments. Lead the evolution of GeoStudio into a broadly adopted platform serving partner teams across Last Mile and globally. Make sound architectural decisions across a diverse portfolio spanning agentic AI interfaces, scalable backend services, tile generation pipelines, reusable React-based frontend components, and web SDKs
- Drive end-to-end delivery of complex engineering projects with clear milestones and measurable outcomes. Maintain a high bar for engineering quality, reliability, and operational excellence across Tier-1 services.
- Build strong working relationships with partner teams across the Geospatial organization, Field Collection Experience, Field Signal Intelligence, and LMAQ. Communicate technical strategy and risks clearly to senior leadership and non-technical stakeholders.
- Expand GeoStudio's reach to new teams and global markets. Ensure the GeoStudio Assistant delivers a high-quality, reliable AI-powered experience for customers. Champion reusable components, SDKs, and APIs that accelerate delivery across the Last Mile technology ecosystem.

A day in the life

- Lead, grow, and develop a team of SDEs, fostering a culture of technical excellence, ownership, and continuous improvement
- Drive the technical roadmap for scaling GeoStudio from a focused editing tool to a platform adopted by partner teams across Last Mile and globally
- Partner closely with teams across the Geospatial organization, Field Collection Experience, Field Signal Intelligence, and Last Mile Analytics and Quality (LMAQ) to align on shared platform direction and deliver integrated solutions
- Own the architecture and delivery of scalable backend systems, tile generation pipelines, reusable frontend component libraries, and AI-powered customer-facing features
- Translate ambiguous business and product requirements into clear engineering plans with well-defined milestones and measurable outcomes
- Establish and uphold engineering standards for reliability, scalability, and operational excellence across Tier-1 services
- Represent your team in cross-functional technical forums and influence the broader geospatial platform strategy
jobs.description_short: Amazon's Last Mile delivery network moves millions of packages every day and the accuracy of the maps powering that network matters at every turn. The Suggested Editing Foundations team sits at the heart

jobs.title: Data Center Manager

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 4+ years of Information Technology (IT) experience, or Bachelor's degree in computer science, engineering, mathematics or equivalent
- 3+ years of operations and on-call support for data center facilities, mission critical plants, or production facilities experience
- 5+ years of computer hardware experience, including troubleshooting and repairing experience
jobs.description:
As a Data Center Manager in Amazon, you will get to manage the world’s largest Cloud Computing Infrastructure. We encounter interesting, challenging & complex problems every day, being a technical manager in Amazon means that you can innovate to solve these issues and help drive Operations Excellence in all areas of your role.

You will be a proven manager with experience managing teams of high performers. You will have the ability to refine and develop processes to optimize operational excellence in every area of your role. You will be able to drive performance levels to achieve the pace we build and operate our Data Centers at. You will have previous experience in Data Centers, either building or managing (or both) along with exposure to developing great relationships with both internal teams and external vendors.

Key job responsibilities
- Managing and developing teams of engineers, providing both technical and leadership expertise and ensuring highest levels of performance.
- Maintaining existing Data Centers
- Helping build and bring online new Data Centers
- Constantly improving all our processes and procedures. We believe there is nothing we cannot improve
- Assisting & managing relationships with external vendors & contractors
- Liaising with internal teams & management groups
- Ensuring we adhere to and exceed local Health & Safety standards in all our Data Centers
- Creating and maintaining metrics on all aspects of our Data Centers and utilizing those metrics to drive positive changes
- Assisting in implementing service methodologies including incident management, problem management, change management, capacity management, etc.

About the team
AWS Infrastructure Services owns the design, planning, delivery, and operation of all AWS global infrastructure. In other words, we’re the people who keep the cloud running. We support all AWS data centers and all of the servers, storage, networking, power, and cooling equipment that ensure our customers have continual access to the innovation they rely on. We work on the most challenging problems, with thousands of variables impacting the supply chain — and we’re looking for talented people who want to help.

You’ll join a diverse team of software, hardware, and network engineers, supply chain specialists, security experts, operations managers, and other vital roles. You’ll collaborate with people across AWS to help us deliver the highest standards for safety and security while providing seemingly infinite capacity at the lowest possible cost for our customers. And you’ll experience an inclusive culture that welcomes bold ideas and empowers you to own them to completion.

Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses.
jobs.description_short: As a Data Center Manager in Amazon, you will get to manage the world’s largest Cloud Computing Infrastructure. We encounter interesting, challenging & complex problems every day, being a technical manager

jobs.title: Data Center Operations Manager, Data Center Ops (DCO)

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- Bachelor's degree in related field
- 5+ years’ experience in product development or related field
- Expert knowledge of food science, techniques, and equipment
- Strong understanding of food manufacturing processes
- Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications
- Experience with product development software and sensory evaluation tools
- Knowledge of food safety regulations and labeling requirements
- Strong project management and organizational skills
- Ability to be in a Store Support office 5 days a week
jobs.description:
The Senior Culinary Product Developer will lead product research, development, and innovation initiatives for Whole Foods Market within the fresh foods categories. This role will be responsible for developing new products from ideation through launch, ensuring adherence to quality standards while maintaining cost efficiency and food safety compliance. This position offers the opportunity to shape the future of our fresh foods categories while working with innovative products and technologies in a dynamic environment.

Key job responsibilities
Lead product development projects from concept to commercialization, including recipe development, testing, and optimization

Conduct product research and competitive analysis to identify market trends and opportunities

Develop and maintain product specifications, preparation instructions, and documentation

Collaborate with cross-functional teams including category, procurement, marketing, and quality assurance

Lead product cuttings, benchmarking, and sensory evaluations

Work with supplier partners on formulation development and first production management

Ensure all products meet company quality standards and ingredient requirements

Provide technical expertise in food science and manufacturing processes

Develop and validate cooking instructions and preparation methods

jobs.title: Data Center Manager

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 1+ years of data center engineering or operations experience
- 4+ years of experience managing people in a technical environment. "
jobs.description:
As a Data Center Manager in Amazon, you will get to manage the world’s largest Cloud Computing Infrastructure. We encounter interesting, challenging & complex problems every day, being a technical manager in Amazon means that you can innovate to solve these issues and help drive Operations Excellence in all areas of your role.

You will be a proven manager with experience managing teams of high performers. You will have the ability to refine and develop processes to optimize operational excellence in every area of your role. You will be able to drive performance levels to achieve the pace we build and operate our Data Centers at. You will have previous experience in Data Centers, either building or managing (or both) along with exposure to developing great relationships with both internal teams and external vendors.

Key job responsibilities
- Managing and developing teams of engineers, providing both technical and leadership expertise and ensuring highest levels of performance.
- Maintaining existing Data Centers
- Helping build and bring online new Data Centers
- Constantly improving all our processes and procedures. We believe there is nothing we cannot improve
- Assisting & managing relationships with external vendors & contractors
- Liaising with internal teams & management groups
- Ensuring we adhere to and exceed local Health & Safety standards in all our Data Centers
- Creating and maintaining metrics on all aspects of our Data Centers and utilizing those metrics to drive positive changes
- Assisting in implementing service methodologies including incident management, problem management, change management, capacity management, etc.

About the team
AWS Infrastructure Services owns the design, planning, delivery, and operation of all AWS global infrastructure. In other words, we’re the people who keep the cloud running. We support all AWS data centers and all of the servers, storage, networking, power, and cooling equipment that ensure our customers have continual access to the innovation they rely on. We work on the most challenging problems, with thousands of variables impacting the supply chain — and we’re looking for talented people who want to help.

You’ll join a diverse team of software, hardware, and network engineers, supply chain specialists, security experts, operations managers, and other vital roles. You’ll collaborate with people across AWS to help us deliver the highest standards for safety and security while providing seemingly infinite capacity at the lowest possible cost for our customers. And you’ll experience an inclusive culture that welcomes bold ideas and empowers you to own them to completion.

Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses.

jobs.description:
Amazon is looking for a leader to fill the position of Senior WW Planning Manager for its global portfolio of device and accessories products. This worldwide planning position is essential to the continued growth of Amazon’s Devices division as it continues its fast paced growth and expansion of its portfolio.

Key job responsibilities
Specifically, the Senior Planning Manager has the following responsibilities:
- Management of our end-to-end planning processes and workflows to drive our build and manufacturing investment decisions to our consumers, retailers, customer replenishment and long-term product development.
- Subject matter expert for demand planning, pricing, promotion, in-stock, procurement, supply chain, logistics, and retailer demand and supply planning processes.
- Management of New Product Launches.
- Owning and communicating the global supply allocation plan to global stakeholders.
- Create and implement inventory allocation strategies to maximize inventory placement, turns, sales, and customer experience.
- Continuously improve key end-to-end planning performance indicators.

A day in the life
As the Senior WW Planning Manager, you own and manage the worldwide planning for specific programs of devices and accessories. It requires the ability to leverage a broad cross-functional organization of business teams and management partners. This role is very visible throughout the organization and this person will lead the generation of large scale and complex plans that are presented to senior leaders and executives.

About the team
The mission of our Worldwide Planning organization is to deliver the most accurate forecasts, inventory targets and allocation plans to ensure every customer is able to purchase the product they want, when and where they want it. In order to achieve this, our team develops and uses optimization tools, advanced statistical models, machine learning algorithms and big data solutions, so we can provide the Device organization with a detailed sales and strategic plan that outlines the opportunities and risks for our customers.

jobs.description:
Amazon is looking for a leader to fill the position of WW Planning Manager for its global portfolio of device and accessories products. This worldwide planning position is essential to the continued growth of Amazon’s Devices division as it continues its fast paced growth and expansion of its portfolio of Fire TVs and Alexa branded products.

The mission of our Worldwide Planning organization is to deliver the most accurate forecasts, inventory targets and allocation plans to ensure every customer is able to purchase the product they want, when and where they want it. In order to achieve this, our team develops and uses optimization tools, advanced statistical models, machine learning algorithms and big data solutions, so we can provide the Device organization with a detailed sales and strategic plan that outlines the opportunities and risks for our customers.



Key job responsibilities
As the WW Planning Manager, you own and manage the worldwide planning for specific programs of devices and accessories. It requires the ability to leverage a broad cross-functional organization of business teams and management partners. This role is very visible throughout the organization and this person will lead the generation of large scale and complex plans that are presented to senior leaders and executives.

Specifically, the WW Planning Manager has the following responsibilities:
- Management of our end-to-end planning processes and workflows to drive our build and manufacturing investment decisions to our consumers, retailers, customer replenishment and long-term product development.
- Subject matter expert for demand planning, pricing, promotion, in-stock, procurement, supply chain, logistics, and retailer demand and supply planning processes.
- Management of New Product Launches.
- Owning and communicating the global supply allocation plan to global stakeholders.
- Create and implement inventory allocation strategies to maximize inventory placement, turns, sales, and customer experience.
- Continuously improve key end-to-end planning performance indicators from Forecast MAPEs to customer on-time delivery.

A day in the life
As the WW Planning Manager, you own and manage the worldwide planning for specific programs of devices and accessories. It requires the ability to leverage a broad cross-functional organization of business teams and management partners. This role is very visible throughout the organization and this person will lead the generation of large scale and complex plans that are presented to senior leaders and executives.

About the team
The mission of our Worldwide Planning organization is to deliver the most accurate forecasts, inventory targets and allocation plans to ensure every customer is able to purchase the product they want, when and where they want it. In order to achieve this, our team develops and uses optimization tools, advanced statistical models, machine learning algorithms and big data solutions, so we can provide the Device organization with a detailed sales and strategic plan that outlines the opportunities and risks for our customers.
